title: Pakistan's powerful military leader Asim Munir is reshaping the country
subhed: In the year since Pakistan's brief conflict with India in May 2025, the head of Pakistan's armed forces has used the war to consolidate his hold on the state, further erasing the distinction between civilian and military power.

title: Raul Castro is still pulling the strings in Cuba
SpaceX sends Starship rocket sailing into space
New Ebola outbreak in the DR Congo puts international solidarity to the test
subhed: Six years after the Covid-19 pandemic, the response to large-scale health crises is being undermined by the collapse of international aid.

title: Italy, France, UK, Germany urge Israel to end settlement expansion
subhed: The joint statement argued that Israel is 'undermining stability,' noting that settler violence towards Palestinians is at 'unprecedented levels.' The statement came at the end of a tense week in European-Israeli relations following the release of footage of European Gaza-bound flotilla activists being forced to kneel, foreheads to the ground and hands tied.

title: 40,000 people under evacuation orders after a chemical tank leak in Southern California
subhed: At an aerospace plastics facility, a storage tank holding methyl methacrylate, a hazardous chemical used to make plastic parts, overheated on Thursday and is now at risk of exploding. The Orange County fire authority pleaded with residents to follow evacuation orders, insisting: 'This thing is going to fail.'

title: The start-ups dreaming of creating genetically modified babies
subhed: Born out of the American tech sector, Preventive and Origin Genomics aim to advance the modification of embryo genomes. This highly controversial and largely banned technique has raised fears of a resurgence of a form of eugenics.

Why Airbus, Air France were convicted on appeal in 2009 Rio-Paris crash
subhed: The two aviation giants were convicted of manslaughter in the accident of flight AF447, which killed 228 people on June 1, 2009. Both companies had previously been found not guilty at the first trial. They are now filing an appeal with France's highest court.

title: Macron opens the door to 'reparations' for slavery
subhed: The French president launched a new chapter in France's reckoning with its past by addressing, for the first time, the issue of 'reparations' linked to the transatlantic slave trade.

title: From Italy to Libya, the dehumanizing processing of migrants
subhed: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (4/4). Exploited, extorted and abused in Libya, exiles who survive the crossing are rendered invisible and sometimes fall into the hands of mafias linked to agricultural businesses. But 'what matters is that irregular arrivals decrease,' as an official in Rome put it.
